text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
It's Valentine's Day, and Manhattan Cafe has
invited me to the empty classroom she's using.

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text:
Right now, right in front of you... there's a box of
chocolates.

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: ...Can you see it?

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
However hard I squint, I can't see anything. I
answer honestly, and...

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: And because you can't see it... it doesn't exist?

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text:
Isn't that a little strange? I mean, you can't see
my friend either, can you?

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: But you believe she exists...

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text:
You said the chocolates don't exist, but you
believe my friend does...

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: So... what's the difference?

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
She does have a point. But after some thinking, I
figure out what the difference might be...

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: Do you... have an answer?
choice_data_list: The difference is that you believe in your friend, Cafe.

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: So, if I believe something exists... you do too?

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: I see...

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: It's because... you're like that, that I...

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: Um, here you go...

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
Manhattan Cafe takes out an actual box of
chocolates.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text: But she doesn't hand it over immediately.

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text:
...Right now, in front of you, there's a box of
chocolates.

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: ...Can you see it?

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
I get the urge to play a little trick on her. I
wonder how she'll react if I say the opposite of
what she expects.

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text:
...It's there. Really. A box of chocolates I made
myself...

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text:
You have to tell the truth... I tried really hard with
these...

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
I've upset her a little. Maybe getting to see that
expression on her face is my second Valentine's
gift.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text: She continues with the questions.

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text:
Do you think these chocolates were made... with
love?

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: Try using your imagination...

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
Manhattan Cafe is shrouded in mystery. I feel like
she wouldn't put her love into some chocolates so
easily.

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: ...They were made with love. You might not be able to see it, but it's there...

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: Because I believe it is.

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: And now... for my last question.

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text:
Once you accept my gift, you can't give it back.
Knowing that... will you take it?

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
I struggle with my decision... And as I mull things
over...

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text: Thump!

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
A powerful force shoves me from behind, as if to
drive me forward.

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: ...Trainer?

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text: I don't think there's any way out.
